title: [release-1.22] Bump c/storage to v1.34.1
body:
Bump c/storage to v1.34.0 for the release-1.22 branch
Also bump go to 1.16 in Makefile and .cirrus.yml

[NO TESTS NEEDED]
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

@nalind @giuseppe This storage failure is difficult to understand.  Something in user namespace or networking changes causing these issues.
where is that failure happening?  CI seems to pass now?

nevermind my last comment, I think the issue is introduced with: https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/b4477c7a8b80ae0c2fe7aa63f20ef8b91d18cfa5

I think we should attempt the fallback path on any error, not only `ENOENT`.  When running in a user namespace, we fix the directory permissions later on (e.g. /var/lib/containers/storage is made `0711`).  The lstat fails when `/var/lib/containers/storage` is still using permissions `0700`.

Opened a PR to address it: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/994
and https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3454 to verify it
